channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mamata Banerjee asserts she will not resign, urges TMC MLAs to protest symbolically
item.description: In Kolkata, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) gathered its newly elected MLAs and senior leaders at Kalighat on Wednesday. West Bengal Chief Minister and party chief Mamata Banerjee, along with national ge...

item.title: President Ruto Appoints Justice Mohamed Warsame to Supreme Court
item.description: President William Ruto has officially appointed Justice Mohamed Abdullahi Warsame to serve as a judge at the Supreme Court of Kenya, filling the vacancy left by the late Justice Mohammed Ibrahim. This...

item.title: Pakistan facilitates historic US-Iran dialogue, promotes regional peace
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has highlighted Pakistan’s role as a key contributor to regional peace and stability during the 6th International Paigham-e-Islam Conference held in Islamabad. He praised...

item.title: New Zealand’s expanding AI data centre industry raises questions over economic control and local benefits
item.description: New Zealand is emerging as a new player in the global race to build infrastructure for artificial intelligence, with major international companies investing heavily in large-scale data centres. One of...

item.title: Electricity consumers to get Rs1.75 per unit reduction in tariff from June to August 2026
item.description: Electricity consumers across Pakistan are set to benefit from a temporary reduction of Rs1.75 per unit in their electricity bills for the next three months, covering June to August 2026. This relief c...

item.title: Quezon City resident wins P15 million in May 6 Megalotto 6/45 draw
item.description: A lucky bettor from Quezon City successfully won the P15 million jackpot in the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) Megalotto 6/45 draw held on Wednesday, May 6, 2026. The winning numbers for...

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Results in Evacuations and Deaths
item.description: A serious outbreak of Hantavirus has occurred on a cruise ship anchored off the coast of Africa, prompting the evacuation of three passengers suspected of being infected. Tragically, three other indiv...

item.title: RCMP Investigation Ongoing Into Bathurst Girl’s Disappearance and Homicide, Five Years On
item.description: The New Brunswick RCMP continues its investigation into the disappearance and homicide of a young girl from Bathurst, more than five years after the tragic events. Law enforcement officials have maint...

item.title: Poll shows Americans disapprove of Trump and Hegseth’s religious messaging
item.description: A recent poll indicates that a significant majority of Americans disapprove of the religious language used by President Donald Trump and Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth. The survey reveals that 87 perc...

item.title: Tanner Horner Sentenced to Death in Athena Strand Murder Case
item.description: Tanner Horner, a former FedEx delivery driver, has been sentenced to death for the killing of 7-year-old Athena Strand in Texas. Horner pleaded guilty to capital murder on the opening day of his trial...

item.title: Simba and Yanga Face Key Matches in Close Tanzanian Premier League Race
item.description: The Mainland Premier League in Tanzania is entering a tense phase as Simba SC and Young Africans (Yanga) prepare for crucial fixtures after their recent 2-2 derby draw in Dar es Salaam. With only ten ...

item.title: High Court in Mwanza Dismisses Advocate's Claim for Sh2.4 Million Legal Fees Due to Lack of Written Agreement
item.description: The High Court at Mwanza Sub-Registry has dismissed an application by Advocate Reagan Charles who wanted to force his former client, Kamugisha Byabato, to pay him Sh2.4 million as legal fees. The two ...

item.title: Resignations Follow TMC's Election Defeat in West Bengal
item.description: Following the significant defeat of Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress (TMC) in the recent West Bengal Assembly elections, multiple former bureaucrats, economists, and advisors have tendered their...

item.title: Breakdown of Kenya’s Fuel Prices and the Role of Taxes, Costs, and Subsidies
item.description: Fuel prices in Kenya shot up in April 2026, bringing back the usual debate about whether taxes are to blame. While some leaders claim that taxes make up about half of the pump price, the actual data s...

item.title: Wike Criticizes Peter Obi Over Frequent Party Switching
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Minister, Nyesom Wike, don drag Peter Obi, former Governor of Anambra State, over him habit of dey waka from one political party go another. During him monthly medi...

item.title: Mombasa Residents Hold Peaceful Protest Over Rising Femicide Cases
item.description: On Wednesday, May 6, 2026, residents of Mombasa, led by the activist group VOCAL Africa, held peaceful demonstrations protesting the rising cases of femicide in Kenya. The demonstrators carried placar...

item.title: Swiss passenger contracts hantavirus after cruise, receiving treatment in Zurich
item.description: A Swiss man who recently travelled on the MV Hondius cruise ship has tested positive for hantavirus and is receiving treatment at University Hospital Zurich. The ship, flying the Dutch flag, departed ...

item.title: Pakistan Navy Highlights Importance of Continuous Operational Readiness and Advanced Maritime Capabilities
item.description: Chief of Naval Staff, Admiral Naveed Ashraf, has highlighted the need for the Pakistan Navy to maintain perpetual combat readiness against both conventional and unconventional threats in the maritime ...

item.title: Government announces $10 billion plan to boost fuel and fertiliser security
item.description: The Australian government has announced a $10 billion plan aimed at strengthening the nation’s fuel and fertiliser security amid rising global tensions and supply concerns. Prime Minister Anthony Alba...

item.title: President Samia Urges New Leaders to Serve Citizens with Integrity and Humility
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has called on newly sworn-in leaders to remember that leadership is a trust, not a platform for self-glorification. During the ceremony at Chamwino State House, she remin...
